ingredients: 4 oz cooked octopus, cut into small pieces
1 1/2 cups dashi (or water plus instant dashi)
2 tbsp pickled red ginger (beni shoga), minced (optional)
Aonori (seaweed flakes) and bonito flakes, for topping
steps: Heat a takoyaki pan over medium heat and generously oil each cavity.
Whisk flour, baking powder, and salt. In another bowl whisk eggs with dashi, then whisk into dry ingredients until just smooth.
Pour batter into cavities, slightly overflowing. Add octopus, scallions, pickled ginger, and tenkasu to each.
When edges set, use skewers to turn each ball 90 degrees; keep turning as it cooks so it forms a round ball and browns evenly (about 8-12 minutes total).
Serve hot topped with takoyaki sauce, Japanese mayo, aonori, and bonito flakes.
tips: Oil the pan well to prevent sticking; keep turning gradually so the uncooked batter becomes the outer shell.
